titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles Hemsley Gick to Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 26 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles Hemsley Gick

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the Charles Hemsley Gick, on behalf of the Irish Medical Association, to the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) regarding the application of the Scotch Act 19165 to Ireland. Gick requests that the RCPI nominate a member to act in conjunction with a committee to consider the necessary amendments.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Irish Medical Association.
FOUNDED 1839; INCORPORATED 1882. Secretary
C. HEMSLEY GICK,
58, DAME STREET,
DUBLIN.
PHONE No 1857.
TELEGRAMS, GICK DUBLIN 1857 Royal College of Surgeons,
Dublin 26th June 1916. Dear Sir, At the Annual meeting of the Irish Medical Association
held on the 21st inst: the following Resolution was passed:-
"That the principles of the Midwives' Acts (England & Scotland)
"be embodied in a special Act suitable for, and made applicable
"to Ireland." I was directed to send above to the Royal Colleges of
Physicians and Surgeons, the Royal Academy of Medicine, the Ulster
and Cork Medical Societies, and the Irish Committee of the British
Medical Association; requesting each to nominate a member to act
in conjunction with a Committee to be appointed by the Council of
the I. M. A. to consider amendments necessary to make the Scotch Act
of 1915 applicable to Ireland. In order that there may be an early meeting of the Committee
(which will be appointed by the Council next Friday) will you kindly
endeavour to let me have the name and address of the nominee from
your Body as soon as possible. Yours faithfully, C Hemsley Gick
Secretary. P.S. Copy of each Act will be sent you in a few days. Macd.C
7.VII.16
